research_topic: Independent review-angle deep research on Phase 010 (sub-phases 001-006), 010/007 remediation (T-A through T-F), and 011 manual-testing-playbook coverage-and-run. Verify correctness, security, observability, and test coverage match shipped code on main. 33 closed findings + 11 new vitest cases under audit. Read code on main directly; do not rely on implementation-summary.md as primary evidence.
key_questions: RQ1: Are there P0/P1 correctness regressions in 010/001-006 the 010/007 remediation missed?
RQ2: Are the 33 010/007 findings (R-007-1..21 + P2-1..12) genuinely closed, or did doc-edit closes paper over real surface gaps?

RQ3: Are 010/007 hardenings (path canonicalization, multi-file diff boundary, control-char sanitization, prompt-injection denylist, limit+1 overflow, multi-subject seed preservation, failureFallback.code, trust-badge merge-per-field, causal-edges generation counter) tight at boundaries — or are reachable adversarial inputs bypass them?
RQ4: Do umbrella docs (root README, SKILL.md, mcp_server README/INSTALL_GUIDE) accurately describe what 010 + 010/007 + 011 shipped?
RQ5: Are the 011 playbook scenarios + 17 new vitest cases sufficient to detect regressions in 010/007 hardening?
non_goals: No code changes during iterations 1-9; iteration 10 may PROPOSE remediation but not implement
Do not modify 010 sub-phase implementation-summary or checklist files (T-B owns those)
Do not re-run 010/007 deep-review iterations (already complete)
Do not treat README claims as sufficient evidence without code/test confirmation
stop_conditions: Stop earlier if every RQ has source-backed answers AND no new P0/P1 findings for 2 consecutive iterations
Escalate rather than continue if state becomes invalid
